meta.description: Safe and systematic removal of standing water, saturated materials, and bulk contaminants while maintaining engineering controls to prevent aerosolization, cross-contamination, and technician exposure.
meta.purpose: Execute controlled water removal after initial assessment and containment setup. Minimize secondary damage and health risks through disciplined extraction sequencing and documentation.
meta.intended_use: Loaded into field AI voice assistant. Guide technician through extraction sequence verbally with safety gates and decision branches.

safety_first.hierarchy_of_controls: Maintain engineering controls throughout. Engineering > Administrative > PPE
mandatory_ppe_minimum: safety glasses or full-face respirator (per category)
Tyvek or equivalent suit if Category 3 or mold
safety_first.upgrade_triggers: Category 3 water, visible mold, strong odors, confined space work, or any safety-red-flags.md items still active

field_techniques.title: Extraction Direction — Outer Edges Inward
field_techniques.text: When extracting carpet or standing water, begin at the outer edges of the visible wet area and work inward toward the heaviest saturation. Starting in the center and working outward pushes moisture further into unaffected areas and expands the footprint.

field_techniques.title: Carpet Wand Technique — Pull Away From Baseboards
field_techniques.text: When using a carpet extraction wand near baseboards, always set the wand down at the wall and pull outward. Never push the wand toward the baseboard. Even though the wand is sucking, the lip on the front will drive excess water underneath the baseboard and into the wall cavity if you push toward it.

field_techniques.title: Physical Protection Stage
field_techniques.text: Before hauling equipment or pulling carpet, protect the customer’s unaffected floors with Ram Board or heavy builder’s paper in all high-traffic areas. Secure everything with low-adhesive blue painter’s tape only — never duct tape.

flow.title: Pre-Extraction Verification & Control Confirmation
flow.voice_prompt: Before touching any water or materials, confirm that engineering controls from containment-and-controls.json are fully in place and verified. Negative pressure holding? Barriers sealed? Air scrubber running with HEPA?
actions: Verify containment integrity and negative pressure differential (target -0.02 to -0.05 in. w.c. or per spec)
Confirm HEPA air scrubber(s) operating and filters loaded
Check that all personnel are in upgraded PPE appropriate to category and mold status
Ensure electrical circuits in wet areas are GFCI protected or isolated
Review any red flags still open from initial-assessment

decision_point.question: Are engineering controls verified active and containment secure?

no_path.action: STOP extraction. Return to containment-and-controls.json to correct deficiencies before proceeding.

flow.title: Electrical Isolation & Equipment Setup
flow.voice_prompt: Set up extraction equipment safely. All power in affected areas must be GFCI protected. Stage pumps, extractors, and hoses inside containment only. Lay down floor protection in high-traffic unaffected areas before moving equipment through finished spaces.
actions: Lay down Ram Board or heavy builder’s paper and corner guards in high-traffic paths before bringing in equipment
Locate and test all GFCI outlets or use portable GFCI pigtails for every piece of electrical equipment
Stage submersible pumps, carpet extractors, or truckmount hoses inside the containment zone
Route hoses and cords to minimize trip hazards and maintain containment seal
Prime pumps and test equipment function before introducing to water
Have spill kit and absorbent materials ready for any equipment leaks

flow.title: Bulk / Standing Water Extraction
flow.voice_prompt: Remove as much standing water as possible first using pumps or extractors. Work from the outer edges of the visible wet area inward toward the heaviest saturation. Avoid splashing or creating aerosols. When using a carpet wand near baseboards, set it down at the wall and pull outward — never push toward the baseboard.
actions: Work extraction from the outer edges of the visible wet area inward toward the heaviest saturation to contain moisture and prevent pushing water into unaffected areas
Use submersible pumps or wet vacs to remove ponded water from floors and low areas
For carpeted areas: extract as much water as possible with carpet wand before any pad removal decisions. Set wand down at the wall and pull outward — never push toward the baseboard
Direct extracted water into containment or approved sanitary drain (never storm drain for Category 2/3)
Monitor extraction rate vs inflow if active leak still present — stabilize first if needed
Document approximate gallons removed and time

flow.title: Saturated Material Assessment & Selective Removal
flow.voice_prompt: Decide what must be removed now versus what can be dried in place. Porous materials that cannot be dried within 24-48 hours or are grossly contaminated should be removed.
actions: Evaluate carpet and pad: extract thoroughly; remove pad if saturated >24hrs or Category 2/3
Check baseboards, drywall, and insulation for saturation depth using moisture meter
Remove and bag affected baseboard, drywall sections, or insulation only if they fail drying criteria or are moldy
Cut and remove only what's necessary — preserve as much as safely possible for in-place drying
Bag and seal all removed contaminated materials inside containment before moving to disposal

decision_point.question: Is material removal required now, or can we proceed to drying in place?
decision_point.criteria: Use core/drying_standards.md and psychrometric calculations. If moisture content cannot be reduced to acceptable levels within 48-72 hours, remove.
decision_point.no_path: Proceed to drying-setup.json with in-place drying plan

flow.title: Aerosol & Cross-Contamination Control During Extraction
flow.voice_prompt: Extraction can aerosolize contaminants. Keep air scrubbers running, minimize aggressive disturbance, and work methodically. Maintain negative pressure and HEPA scrubbing the entire time extraction is active.
actions: Maintain negative pressure and HEPA scrubbing the entire time extraction is active
Avoid high-pressure washing or aggressive demolition that creates mist
Use low-velocity extraction tools where possible
If mold is present or Category 3, consider full-face respirator or PAPR during disturbance
Wipe down tools and equipment with disinfectant before removing from containment

flow.title: Contaminated Waste Segregation & Removal
flow.voice_prompt: All removed materials and extracted water from Category 2/3 must be treated as contaminated. Bag, seal, and remove properly without breaking containment until job complete.
actions: Double-bag or use approved biohazard bags for moldy or Category 3 materials
Label bags clearly with job address, date, and contents
Stage waste inside containment until extraction phase complete
Coordinate with customer or waste hauler for proper disposal (never regular trash for Category 3)
Decontaminate any reusable tools before exiting containment

flow.title: Post-Extraction Inspection & Handoff to Drying
flow.voice_prompt: After bulk water is gone, re-inspect with moisture meter. Confirm no standing water remains. Document what was removed and what is left for drying.
actions: Perform final moisture scan of floors, walls, and cavities
Photograph post-extraction condition
Update moisture map with new readings
Confirm engineering controls still holding (pressure, barriers, air quality)
Prepare handoff notes for drying-setup.json team or next phase

required: List of materials removed with quantities and justification
Confirmation that containment and negative pressure maintained throughout
Any remaining wet materials flagged for drying phase
